narrative:
The Karnataka High Court achieved a 46.6% overall hearing rate on July 21, 2026, resolving 1,122 of the 2,407 scheduled cases across 42 judges. This marks a steady rise in efficiency compared to the previous day’s hearing rate of 44.5% (1,165 of 2,620 cases) and is significantly higher than the 40.0% rate recorded on July 16, though it remains below the 49.2% peak from July 17.

Among individual benches, Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the court's highest caseload, hearing 110 of 254 scheduled cases (43.3%). Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 also handled substantial volume, hearing 86 of 153 cases (56.2%) in his largest list. Notable high-efficiency performances were led by Justice Shivashankar Amarannavar in Hall 20, who cleared 56 of 69 cases (81.2%)—maintaining a strong clearance level consistent with his 81.4% rate on July 20—and Justice V. Srishananda in Hall 21, who resolved 80 of 103 cases (77.7%).

Stage-wise analysis reveals that "Orders" remained the most prominent stage, with 462 of 882 scheduled cases heard (52.4%), indicating improved conversion from the prior day's 46.7%. "Further Hearing" achieved the highest conversion rate among major stages at 73.5% (86 of 117), while "Final Hearing" converted at 23.7% (123 of 519), a minor improvement over yesterday's 21.0%.

By case type, Writ Petitions (WP) continued to dominate the docket, with 438 of 1,055 cases heard (41.5%), representing a minor increase from yesterday's 40.0% conversion rate. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) also saw strong progress, clearing 210 of 390 cases (53.8%), while Regular Second Appeals (RSA) achieved a high clearance rate of 70.4%, resolving 88 of 125 scheduled matters.
